title: Ensure 'Access Transparency' is 'Enabled'

input_row.title: Ensure 'Access Transparency' is 'Enabled'
input_row.description: GCP Access Transparency provides audit logs for all actions that Google personnel take in your Google Cloud resources.
input_row.rationale: Controlling access to your information is one of the foundations of information security. Given that Google Employees do have access to your organizations' projects for support reasons, you should have logging in place to view who, when, and why your information is being accessed.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Determine if Access Transparency is Enabled 1. From the Google Cloud Home, click on the Navigation hamburger menu in the top left. Hover over the IAM & Admin Menu. Select settings in the middle of the column that opens. 2. The status will be under the heading Access Transparency. Status should be Enabled
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Add privileges to enable Access Transparency 1. From the Google Cloud Home, within the project you wish to check, click on the Navigation hamburger menu in the top left. Hover over the 'IAM and Admin'. Select IAM in the top of the column that opens. 2. Click the blue button the says +add at the top of the screen. 3. In the principals field, select a user or group by typing in their associated email address. 4. Click on the role field to expand it. In the filter field enter Access Transparency Admin and select it. 5. Click save. Verify that the Google Cloud project is associated with a billing account 1. From the Google Cloud Home, click on the Navigation hamburger menu in the top left. Select Billing. 2. If you see This project is not associated with a billing account you will need to enter billing information or switch to a project with a billing account. Enable Access Transparency 1. From the Google Cloud Home, click on the Navigation hamburger menu in the top left. Hover over the IAM & Admin Menu. Select settings in the middle of the column that opens. 2. Click the blue button labeled Enable Access Transparency for Organization
input_row.impact: To use Access Transparency your organization will need to have at one of the following support level: Premium, Enterprise, Platinum, or Gold. There will be subscription costs associated with support, as well as increased storage costs for storing the logs. You will also not be able to turn Access Transparency off yourself, and you will need to submit a service request to Google Cloud Support.
input_row.default_value: By default Access Transparency is not enabled.
input_row.additional_information: To enable Access Transparency for your Google Cloud organization, your Google Cloud organization must have one of the following customer support levels: Premium, Enterprise, Platinum, or Gold.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control requires checking whether Access Transparency is enabled in GCP, which can be queried using GCP APIs. The description, rationale, audit, and remediation fields indicate that it is a standard service property that can be verified programmatically.

- GCP APIs can query Access Transparency settings.
- The audit instructions provide specific steps that imply available API endpoints or command-line commands within the gcloud SDK.
- As there are no requirements for SSH access or manual inspections based on the control, this supports automated verification.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_access_transparency_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
To automate the compliance check for Access Transparency:

1. Use the GCP API or the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) to check the status of Access Transparency:
   - GCP API call: Query the organization-wide settings related to Access Transparency.
   - GCP SDK (gcloud) command: Utilize `gcloud alpha access-transparency settings describe` or similar commands that retrieve settings related to Access Transparency.

2. Verify that Access Transparency is enabled:
   - Expected output: The settings response should indicate that Access Transparency is enabled; if it is not, the response should reflect that Access Transparency is disabled.

3. Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
   - Pass Criteria: The `enabled` property for Access Transparency must be `true`.
   - Fail Criteria: The `enabled` property for Access Transparency must be `false`.

This approach leverages existing Google Cloud resources and APIs to verify compliance automatically without manual intervention.
